cards.back: A scheduling algorithm in which the CPU executes the processes in the order they arrive.
A scheduling algorithm that assigns a fixed time slice (time quantum) to each process, and the CPU switches between processes after each time slice.
A scheduling algorithm that assigns a priority to each process, and the CPU executes the process with the highest priority first.
The process of saving the current state of a process and restoring the state of the next process to be executed by the CPU.
A situation in which two or more processes are unable to proceed because each is waiting for the other to release a resource.
